untranslatable_reason: This signal requires real-time multi-market price divergence detection (cross-exchange arbitrage), bid-ask spread tracking across venues, tick-level persistence validation, and execution timing synchronization. These require market microstructure data and multi-venue connectivity not expressible through single-market OHLCV indicators. The signal also requires: (1) tracking price differences across two separate markets simultaneously, (2) monitoring bid-ask spreads in both markets, (3) counting consecutive ticks of divergence persistence, and (4) execution logic tied to fill status across multiple venues. No single-market indicator from the allowed list can capture these cross-venue relationships.
translation_notes: Signal describes a multi-market arbitrage strategy requiring real-time divergence monitoring across exchanges. The allowed indicator set is limited to single-market technical indicators (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, etc.) and cannot express: (a) price_divergence_pct between two markets, (b) bid_ask_spread comparisons across venues, (c) tick persistence counters, or (d) multi-venue execution synchronization. A proper implementation would need custom market microstructure indicators tracking market depth, order book imbalances, and cross-exchange quote feeds. The 0.5% divergence threshold, 2-tick persistence requirement, and spread ratio condition are not translatable to the available single-market indicator vocabulary.
